title: I bought two Cardputer ADVs with the

messages.author: SimonJ
messages.text: My LoRa caps just arrived yesterday, and I did the same confirmation as you. I haven't had time to try to get MeshCore up and running on them, but am hoping to have some time to start messing with it this weekend.
messages.content_html: My LoRa caps just arrived yesterday, and I did the same confirmation as you. I haven't had time to try to get MeshCore up and running on them, but am hoping to have some time to start messing with it this weekend.
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
Mumonkan
i also have the cardputer adv + lora cap. can confirm the meshtastic firmware works as one might expect. but obviously (? since i am here haha) i am more interested in running meshcore on it. i have found very little info on someone getting it working with the lora cap.

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: Someone here - I forget the Discord name - works with MToolsTec - has created a custom Meshcore firmware that runs on the Cardputer Adv. Will try find a link
messages.content_html: Someone here - I forget the Discord name - works with MToolsTec - has created a custom Meshcore firmware that runs on the Cardputer Adv. Will try find a link
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Mumonkan
messages.text: thanks. i have tried this, but it seems to only work with external lora devices, that is to say, not the lora cap made by m5stack. hopefully they will add support.
messages.content_html: thanks. i have tried this, but it seems to only work with external lora devices, that is to say, not the lora cap made by m5stack. hopefully they will add support.

messages.author: Mumonkan
messages.text: update! the latest version now supports the LoRa cap there is still some problem with using the gps, but i am now able to use the cardputer + cap as a portable meshcore node. big thanks to the developer for the update.
messages.content_html: update! the latest version now supports the LoRa cap there is still some problem with using the gps, but i am now able to use the cardputer + cap as a portable meshcore node. big thanks to the developer for the update.

messages.author: Keegan The Veganator
messages.text: I think im going to buy one of these cardputer adv devices... it seems like the cheapest way to get a lora pager... plus the cardputer looks interesting for all kinds of other littke fun hacky projects... do you know if meshcore on it has message history? i noticed the meshtastic verison does not so if you miss a message- its gone... having an sd card it seems like a no brainer to me that there should be something needing to be implemented to store messages
messages.content_html: I think im going to buy one of these cardputer adv devices... it seems like the cheapest way to get a lora pager... plus the cardputer looks interesting for all kinds of other littke fun hacky projects... do you know if meshcore on it has message history? i noticed the meshtastic verison does not so if you miss a message- its gone... having an sd card it seems like a no brainer to me that there should be something needing to be implemented to store messages
messages.reply_html:
Mumonkan
update! the latest version now supports the LoRa cap there is still some problem with using the gps, but i am now able to use the cardputer + cap as a portable meshcore node. big thanks to the developer for the update.

messages.author: Mumonkan
messages.text: yes, messages appear as a little stack, showing 1 or 2 depending on how long they are. you can scroll back with arrow keys.
messages.content_html: yes, messages appear as a little stack, showing 1 or 2 depending on how long they are. you can scroll back with arrow keys.

messages.author: Mumonkan
messages.text: public channel (changed screen colors). you basically only have room for a single message with user name. but you scroll back and forth with arrows.
messages.content_html: public channel (changed screen colors). you basically only have room for a single message with user name. but you scroll back and forth with arrows.

messages.author: Keegan The Veganator
messages.text: ok cheers for the info dude! much appreciated... i think it seems a bit better than the meshtastic option currently - however there does seem to be some room for improvement... i think im gonna order one and maybe see if i can use claude to help me tweak the code - all the info is open, about how to access all the hardware on the device so it should be possible to add persistent settings and a more robust message history to the sd card. its a very interesting device and i can see it being a problem having to flash and start again everytime you want to test out something else... its one thing flashing a basic firmware... but it would be much better if there was access to a user data folder or something... food for thought! thanks again for taking the time!
messages.content_html: ok cheers for the info dude! much appreciated... i think it seems a bit better than the meshtastic option currently - however there does seem to be some room for improvement... i think im gonna order one and maybe see if i can use claude to help me tweak the code - all the info is open, about how to access all the hardware on the device so it should be possible to add persistent settings and a more robust message history to the sd card. its a very interesting device and i can see it being a problem having to flash and start again everytime you want to test out something else... its one thing flashing a basic firmware... but it would be much better if there was access to a user data folder or something... food for thought! thanks again for taking the time!
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Mumonkan
messages.text: there is a launcher app, which makes it easier to switch around -- but i am unclear as to whether that is just resetting everything each time. i should mess with it. also, fwiw, i put in a "feature request" issue for the persistence thing. maybe if you get somewhere, you can do a pull request to make it part of the official thing and close my ticket. haha
messages.content_html: there is a launcher app, which makes it easier to switch around -- but i am unclear as to whether that is just resetting everything each time. i should mess with it. also, fwiw, i put in a "feature request" issue for the persistence thing. maybe if you get somewhere, you can do a pull request to make it part of the official thing and close my ticket. haha

messages.author: Keegan The Veganator
messages.text: Yeah the launcher app from what I can tell will reflash various .bin firmware files essentially making it so that loading what I would see as a different app is actually loading a complete firmware... However I still think it's possible to modify the meshcore firmware in such a way where it can read and write certain things to another part of an sdcar for the functions we require... Maybe even load up like a private identifier key, so you always appear on the network as the same device.. storing contacts, messages... There has to be a way to flesh it out
messages.content_html: Yeah the launcher app from what I can tell will reflash various .bin firmware files essentially making it so that loading what I would see as a different app is actually loading a complete firmware... However I still think it's possible to modify the meshcore firmware in such a way where it can read and write certain things to another part of an sdcar for the functions we require... Maybe even load up like a private identifier key, so you always appear on the network as the same device.. storing contacts, messages... There has to be a way to flesh it out

messages.text: the maintainer responded positively to my feature request about saving config so maybe it will be in a future release.

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: I use the Launcher firmware with the TDeck Pro and if you go to the launcher CFG menu and backup spiffs, then do the same thing again and backup FAT VF, it saves my node IDs, contacts etc for both the ripple firmware I have installed and the community firmware. I'd be curious if there's a similar option for the Cardputer as well
messages.content_html: I use the Launcher firmware with the TDeck Pro and if you go to the launcher CFG menu and backup spiffs, then do the same thing again and backup FAT VF, it saves my node IDs, contacts etc for both the ripple firmware I have installed and the community firmware. I'd be curious if there's a similar option for the Cardputer as well
messages.reply_html:
Keegan The Veganator
Yeah the launcher app from what I can tell will reflash various .bin firmware files essentially making it so that loading what I would see as a different app is actually loading a complete firmware... However I still think it's possible to modify the meshcore firmware in such a way where it can read and write certain things to another part of an sdcar for the functions we require... Maybe even load up like a private identifier key, so you always appear on the network as the same device.. storing contacts, messages... There has to be a way to flesh it out

messages.author: Keegan The Veganator
messages.text: I won't be able to verrify any functions... I haven't actually bought one yet, just doing research on how it all works before I buy one... I saw a video showing meshtastic and how it didnt save messages to the SD card... In fact it didn't even save messages beyond the next message coming in... That's why I've kinda held off... It would be awesome if they take the cardputer meshcore firmware seriously and make it work with robust persistence... I am however interest in your t-deck... I didn't know you could have a launcher ... I'm assuming you can easily switch between meshtastic and meshcore aswell with this?
messages.content_html: I won't be able to verrify any functions... I haven't actually bought one yet, just doing research on how it all works before I buy one... pelgraine
I use the Launcher firmware with the TDeck Pro and if you go to the launcher CFG menu and backup spiffs, then do the same thing again and backup FAT VF, it saves my node IDs, contacts etc for both the ripple firmware I have installed and the community firmware. I'd be curious if there's a similar option for the Cardputer as well

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: That's right! The Launcher firmware works on both the T-Deck Plus and T-Deck Pro with a SD card. Easy to swap between firmware - just reboot the device and select the other firmware bin. It says install every time but after the first time that process is super quick and if you've backed up as I mentioned above, swapping between firmwares will reload your previously saved config/IDs etc. Check out https://bmorcelli.github.io/Launcher/
messages.content_html: That's right! The Launcher firmware works on both the T-Deck Plus and T-Deck Pro with a SD card. Easy to swap between firmware - just reboot the device and select the other firmware bin. It says install every time but after the first time that process is super quick and if you've backed up as I mentioned above, swapping between firmwares will reload your previously saved config/IDs etc. Check out https://bmorcelli.github.io/Launcher/
embeds_html:
Launcher Project | Web Tools & Firmware Catalog
Launcher firmware tools for M5Stack, LilyGO and ESP32 devices.
messages.reply_html:
Keegan The Veganator
I won't be able to verrify any functions... I haven't actually bought one yet, just doing research on how it all works before I buy one... I saw a video showing meshtastic and how it didnt save messages to the SD card... In fact it didn't even save messages beyond the next message coming in... That's why I've kinda held off... It would be awesome if they take the cardputer meshcore firmware seriously and make it work with robust persistence... I am however interest in your t-deck... I didn't know you could have a launcher ... I'm assuming you can easily switch between meshtastic and meshcore aswell with this?

messages.author: Keegan The Veganator
messages.text: oh well that makes the t-deck's much more interesting to me now ye! i still think ill end up getting the cardputer first though because it has other modules and hackery things i kinda want to play with... but the fact you can switch between the different mesh messengers is a gamechanger... one thing im kinda noticing with meshcore... everyone seems to be running the eu narrow - and it seems to be just because andy kirby told us all too haha... but where im living in wales... one of the longer range options would probably be better ... i kinda got the jist from youtube videos - that meshcore was created because of a congestion issue with meshtastic. and im sure in cities or built up parts of england, the current setup works great- but here ive still not managed to make a contact... im looking at buying a second device to accompany my gat562 30s (1w device) just so i can talk to myself hahaha... the idea of being able to easily switch to meshtastic and at least check the long fast band sounds really appealing... cheers for the info!
messages.content_html: oh well that makes the t-deck's much more interesting to me now ye! i still think ill end up getting the cardputer first though because it has other modules and hackery things i kinda want to play with... but the fact you can switch between the different mesh messengers is a gamechanger... one thing im kinda noticing with meshcore... everyone seems to be running the eu narrow - and it seems to be just because andy kirby told us all too haha... but where im living in wales... one of the longer range options would probably be better ... i kinda got the jist from youtube videos - that meshcore was created because of a congestion issue with meshtastic. and im sure in cities or built up parts of england, the current setup works great- but here ive still not managed to make a contact... im looking at buying a second device to accompany my gat562 30s (1w device) just so i can talk to myself hahaha... the idea of being able to easily switch to meshtastic and at least check the long fast band sounds really appealing... cheers for the info!

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: Welcome! Also worth having a look at the Launcher page to see what other firmware is available for the Cardputer that you could sideload - https://bmorcelli.github.io/Launcher/catalog.html There's not much for the T-Deck Pro yet so I'm going to have to try building it myself
